title: A conservative update preserves total mass under a periodic boundary predicate

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the flux satisfies the periodic-boundary predicate flux(n)=flux(0), then the conservative update preserves the total over the first n cells.
informal.latex: If \(F_n=F_0\) under the periodic-boundary predicate, then \(\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}u_i^+=\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}u_i\).
assumptions: u and flux are real-valued functions on natural-number grid indices
informal.expected_conclusion: The total after the conservative update equals the original total.

declarations.name: periodicBoundary
declarations.statement: def periodicBoundary (flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Prop := flux n = flux 0

declarations.name: totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_periodicBoundary
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_periodicBoundary (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : periodicBoundary flux n) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Translate the periodic-boundary total-preservation theorem into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_periodicBoundary (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : periodicBoundary flux n) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Given periodicBoundary and totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by applying totalOn_conservativeUpdate_of_boundary_eq to the periodic-boundary hypothesis.
